text: Agile methodology is a project management approach commonly used in software development. Work is divided into short cycles called sprints, usually lasting two weeks. Each day starts with a standup—a brief meeting where team members share what they did yesterday, what they will do today, and any obstacles they face. The team maintains a backlog, which is a prioritized list of tasks. Requirements are written as user stories, brief descriptions from the end user's perspective. Instead of finishing everything at once, Agile follows iterative development: teams release small, working parts of the product regularly, gather feedback, and make improvements. This helps teams adapt quickly to changes and deliver value consistently.
